theme: airlock protocol chatter from a derelict ship
title: Airlock Splice: Recovering a Scrambled Counter Relay
strategies: trivial: submit the 'unscrambled, unit-magnitude' guess (inc=A, dec=B, flip=C, noop=D, u=1, p0=+1) ignoring the logged bursts entirely -- this is exactly the checker's own baseline construction, so it reproduces Ratio = 0.1 by construction

greedy: the obvious first read of the log -- run an ordinary least-squares regression of the drift reading y against the four raw per-burst symbol counts (cA,cB,cC,cD) plus an intercept (a bag-of-symbols model that is completely order-blind and never models a persistent polarity flag). Read off the role assignment from the regression coefficients (most-positive coefficient -> inc, most-negative -> dec, remaining two sorted by |coefficient| -> flip/noop by convention), round the inc/dec coefficient spread to an integer unit, and always assume p0=+1 since the model has no notion of a flip-triggered sign inversion. Because logged training bursts are short (<=20 symbols) and rarely contain more than 0-2 polarity-flip pulses, this order-blind fit tracks the noisy training rows reasonably well -- an unconstrained OLS fit is, after all, explicitly minimizing training residuals. But the recovered model has no persistent-state memory: on the long held-out bursts (length 100-500), especially the role-sorted 'clustered' ones where many flips reorder large blocks of nudges, its prediction is a near-constant function of raw symbol counts while the true drift depends critically on the ORDER of flips relative to nudges, so it diverges badly on most tests (it occasionally gets lucky when the regression coefficients happen to already separate the four roles correctly)

strong: recognize that only 4! (roles) x (U_MAX-U_MIN+1) (magnitude) x 2 (starting polarity) candidate automata exist -- a small, exactly-enumerable hypothesis space -- rather than fitting a continuous, order-blind surrogate. Brute-force every role permutation of A/B/C/D crossed with every magnitude and starting polarity, SIMULATE each candidate automaton exactly on every logged training burst (full string, honoring the persistent polarity flag), and score each candidate by total absolute deviation from the logged (possibly noisy) readings. The true wiring is the unique candidate whose deterministic simulation stays close to dozens of independently-drawn noisy logs across a range of flip counts and lengths -- any wrong role assignment or magnitude accumulates large, systematic deviations on rows containing flips, so the minimum-total-error candidate recovers the true wiring with overwhelming reliability. Because the recovered automaton is simulated exactly (not curve-fit), it extrapolates correctly to bursts of any length, including the clustered trap bursts

headroom above strong: the reference `strong` solution already reaches the exact wiring on every test (the true wiring is the unique candidate consistent with dozens of independent logged bursts, so exhaustive enumeration finds it reliably); the checker's eps-floor deliberately caps even that perfect recovery at Ratio = 0.9 rather than 1.0 (matching the anti-saturation convention used elsewhere in this corpus, e.g. fsx_A_0831), so the ceiling never reads as a solved benchmark even though, within this fixed scoring formula, 0.9 is also the mathematical maximum any submission can reach once the small (24 x magnitude x 2) hypothesis space is being searched exhaustively -- headroom here is about avoiding literal 1.0 saturation, not about a further-improvable numeric target beyond exact recovery
